display.purpose: Page titles only, one per page.

body.purpose: Draft text, labels, reading content. Cap at 70ch measure.

label.purpose: Form labels, button text, nav items.

data.purpose: Costs, timestamps, run IDs, model names — tabular/numeric only.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Toasts and popovers only — anything floating above document flow via position: fixed.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out-quart for hover/focus state transitions, 150ms.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action on a view (Save, Run Now, Copy & Mark Posted).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.58 0.10 228); color: oklch(0.97 0.01 80); padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; border: none; font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.48 0.09 228); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.58 0.10 228); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — edit, regenerate, cancel. The default button everywhere that isn't the one primary action.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.93 0.012 80); padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.18 0.007 75); border-color: oklch(0.27 0.006 75); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.58 0.10 228); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Discard, only. Never filled — a filled red button reads as an alarm this product doesn't need to sound.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: transparent; color: oklch(0.62 0.16 25); padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: oklch(0.62 0.16 25 / 0.12); } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.62 0.16 25); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Draft Card
components.description: The one legitimate card in the system — a single drafted post in the Review Workspace.
components.html:
Draft 1 of 3

Sample drafted post text goes here, wrapping naturally across a few lines to show the reading measure.
components.css: .ds-card { background: oklch(0.14 0.006 75); border-radius: 10px; padding: 20px; max-width: 70ch; } .ds-card-meta { font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: oklch(0.60 0.008 78); margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: oklch(0.93 0.012 80); margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field — API keys, source URLs, settings values.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(0.14 0.006 75); color: oklch(0.93 0.012 80); border: 1px solid oklch(0.27 0.006 75); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 14px; font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; width: 100%; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.60 0.008 78); } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.58 0.10 228); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.58 0.10 228 / 0.20); }

components.name: New-Draft Badge
components.description: The 'new/unreviewed' marker on a draft, and the content-safety linter's warning flag. Ember is reserved for exactly these personal/attention moments.
components.css: .ds-badge-accent { display: inline-block; background: oklch(0.68 0.15 45); color: oklch(0.98 0.01 70); padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 6px; font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Single-row nav, active route underlined in primary. No sidebar, no pill background.
components.html: ReviewAPI ConfigSettingsCosts
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 24px; align-items: center; padding: 14px 24px; background: oklch(0.14 0.006 75); border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(0.27 0.006 75); } .ds-nav-link { font-family: Figtree, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: oklch(0.60 0.008 78); text-decoration: none; padding-bottom: 4px; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: oklch(0.93 0.012 80); } .ds-nav-link-active { color: oklch(0.58 0.10 228); border-bottom-color: oklch(0.58 0.10 228); }

narrative.overview: A single warm reading lamp on in an otherwise dark, quiet room — that's the whole system in one image. sift is checked by exactly one person, most likely at the end of a day, to read a handful of drafted posts, glance at what they cost, and either send them or let them go. The room (the interface) stays out of the way — near-black, unlit, structurally quiet — so the one lit thing (the draft text, the number that matters, the action you're actually here to take) is what the eye lands on. This explicitly rejects the cold ops-dashboard look and the generic SaaS-product look. Warmth comes from a genuinely warm off-white ink color, a warm ember accent reserved for personal moments, and a rounded humanist typeface — never from a beige/cream surface.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black, zero-chroma background — the room is dark, not navy, not charcoal-blue.
One structural color (a deep, muted cobalt) used sparingly for navigation and primary actions.
One warm color (a terracotta ember) reserved for moments genuinely personal to the user.
Cards used only where content is genuinely discrete and comparable (draft posts in Review).
Flat by default; depth comes from tonal layering, not shadows.

rules.name: The One Lit Thing Rule
rules.body: Any given screen gets exactly one accent-colored element competing for attention, never several.

rules.name: The No Warm Surface Rule
rules.body: Warmth lives in ink and accent, never in bg. If a background starts reading as brown or cream, its chroma is too high.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Every weight and size on screen comes from the Figtree family. A second sans-serif appearing means the system has drifted.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: A card sits at rest with zero shadow. Shadows appear only on elements that float above document flow.

rules.name: The Card-Is-Not-Default Rule
rules.body: A card is earned by content that is a standalone, comparable unit. Settings sections and cost summaries are a headline and spacing, not a card.

dos: Do keep bg at exactly oklch(0.09 0 0) — zero chroma, no navy or brown tint.
Do spend accent (Ember) only on genuinely personal moments.
Do use tonal layering for depth, never a shadow on ordinary at-rest content.
Do cap draft/body text at ~70ch measure.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every transition.
donts: Don't tint the background warm for coziness — that's the cream/sand AI cliché.
Don't wrap Settings, API Config, or Costs sections in cards.
Don't use a filled red button for Discard.
Don't add glassmorphism or a second typeface alongside Figtree.
Don't put a colored border stripe on any card or list item.
Don't add an uppercase tracked eyebrow label above section headings.
